claimText: Red Sanders is native to the Southern Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh.
evidenceExcerpt: Native to Southern Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh.
examUse: Statement-based question on the natural habitat and endemism of Red Sanders.
distractorHooks: Tamil Nadu or Kerala as primary native state

claimText: Red Sanders grows in dry deciduous forests at altitudes between 150-900 meters.
evidenceExcerpt: Grows in dry deciduous forests between 150-900 meters altitude.
examUse: Statement-based question on the forest type required for Red Sanders growth.

claimText: The characteristic red colour of Red Sanders is imparted by the presence of the red dye 'Santalin'.
evidenceExcerpt: Presence of red dye 'Santalin' imparts its characteristic red colour.
examUse: Fact-based statement or correct-pair question matching plant species with their active compounds/dyes.

claimText: The IUCN conservation status of Red Sanders is Endangered (EN) and it is listed in CITES Appendix II.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Status: CITES Appendix II; IUCN Status: EN (Endangered).
examUse: Correct-pair question on species and their IUCN/CITES status.

claimText: The National Biodiversity Authority sanctioned funds for Red Sanders Conservation under the Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) Mechanism.
evidenceExcerpt: National Biodiversity Authority sanctioned fund for Red Sanders Conservation under Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) Mechanism.
examUse: Statement-based question on the application of the Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) Mechanism.
distractorHooks: Compensatory Afforestation Fund Management and Planning Authority (CAMPA)
